title: Fixing a React Memoized Component Re-renders Due to Changing Object References

statement.markdown: A React-based web application is using memoization to optimize the performance of its components. However, a component is re-rendering unnecessarily when the object references in its dependencies change. Your task is to fix the bug and optimize the component's rendering.
statement.inputFormat: The application's state and props as JSON objects
statement.notes: The component should re-render only when the object itself changes, not when the reference changes.

examples.explanation: The component should re-render only when the object itself changes.

hints.text: Use the `JSON.stringify` method to compare object values

editorial.title: Editorial: Fixing a React Memoized Component Re-renders Due to Changing Object References
editorial.intuition: Memoization in React is used to optimize the performance of components by re-rendering them only when the dependencies change. However, when the object references change, the component re-renders unnecessarily.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach is to check each dependency individually and compare its values.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution is to use the `JSON.stringify` method to compare object values.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use the `JSON.stringify` method to compare object values and re-render the component only when the object itself changes.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is that the component re-renders only when the object itself changes, not when the reference changes.
